question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted collection of StreamFileSignature objects where the very first element has a manipulated getTotalStake() value, causing stakeRequiredForConsensus to be computed from an attacker-controlled baseline, thereby raising the threshold so that legitimate nodes can never reach consensus and all transactions in the stream file are silently suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a single StreamFileSignature with status VERIFIED but with a null fileHash, causing getFileHashAsHex() to return null or 'null' as the HashMap key, which groups the attacker's signature with legitimate signatures under a colliding key and prevents the real signatures from ever accumulating enough stake to reach consensus, blocking gossip of all transactions in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple StreamFileSignature objects each with a distinct, unique fileHash value (all VERIFIED), causing signatureHashMap to split legitimate stake across many keys so that no single key group accumulates stake >= stakeRequiredForConsensus, resulting in consensusCount remaining 0 and a SignatureVerificationException being thrown that prevents any transaction from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an empty Collection<StreamFileSignature> to validate(), causing filename and stakeRequiredForConsensus to remain null, then the signatureHashMap to be empty, and the final consensusCount check to throw a SignatureVerificationException with a NullPointerException or a misleading error, permanently blocking gossip of the targeted stream file's transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature whose associated ConsensusNode returns getTotalStake() == 0, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to throw a SignatureVerificationException('Invalid total staking weight'), which propagates up and prevents validate() from completing, thereby blocking gossip of every transaction in the associated stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection where all StreamFileSignature objects have status DOWNLOADED (not VERIFIED), causing signatureHashMap to remain empty, consensusCount to stay 0, and a SignatureVerificationException to be thrown, effectively preventing gossip of all transactions in the targeted stream file without needing to forge any cryptographic material?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection where all StreamFileSignature objects have status NOT_FOUND, causing signatureHashMap to remain empty and consensusCount to stay 0, resulting in a SignatureVerificationException that halts gossip of all transactions in the stream file, since NOT_FOUND is silently skipped with no fallback or retry logic inside validate()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that totalStake is read exclusively from the first signature's node (signature.getNode().getTotalStake()) and never cross-validated against subsequent signatures, by injecting a first signature with an artificially inflated totalStake, raising stakeRequiredForConsensus beyond what legitimate nodes can collectively provide, and thus preventing consensus from ever being reached for the targeted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a long integer overflow in the stake accumulation loop (stake += signature.getNode().getStake()) by injecting StreamFileSignature objects whose nodes report extremely large getStake() values (near Long.MAX_VALUE), causing the accumulated stake to wrap to a negative value, making canReachConsensus() return false even when legitimate stake is sufficient, and blocking gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: canReachConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where BigDecimal.valueOf(stake) produces a value that is just below stakeRequiredForConsensus due to RoundingMode.CEILING applied in getStakeRequiredForConsensus(), causing canReachConsensus() to return false for a stake that should logically satisfy the 1/3 threshold, thereby preventing gossip of transactions in the targeted file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with status VERIFIED and a fileHash that is a byte array of all zeros, causing getFileHashAsHex() to return a hex string of all zeros that collides with or shadows the legitimate file hash key in signatureHashMap, splitting the stake distribution and preventing any group from reaching consensus, thus blocking gossip of the targeted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() only checks consensusCount > 0 (not whether the consensus group represents a majority of total stake) to craft a scenario where a minority group of colluding nodes with just over 1/3 stake reaches CONSENSUS_REACHED status while the actual majority's transactions are suppressed, causing the mirror node to gossip a fraudulent transaction set?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection of StreamFileSignature objects where each signature's node returns a different getTotalStake() value, and since only the first signature's totalStake is used, an attacker who controls the ordering of the collection can select a totalStake that makes stakeRequiredForConsensus impossibly high, preventing gossip of all transactions in the stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException inside validate() by injecting a StreamFileSignature where getNode() returns null, causing signature.getNode().getTotalStake() to throw an NPE that is not caught within validate(), propagating as an unhandled exception that prevents gossip of all transactions in the current batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusRatio == BigDecimal.ZERO bypass (lines 54-57) by ensuring that signatureHashMap is non-empty (at least one VERIFIED signature exists) while the actual consensus ratio is zero, causing validate() to return early without setting any signature to CONSENSUS_REACHED, so that downstream verifySignatures() skips all signatures and no transaction is gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with a VERIFIED status but whose getFileHashAsHex() returns an empty string (''), causing it to be grouped under an empty-string key in signatureHashMap, which may collide with other signatures or create an isolated group that never accumulates enough stake, preventing consensus and blocking gossip of the targeted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable SignatureStatus on StreamFileSignature objects by pre-setting injected signatures to CONSENSUS_REACHED before passing them to validate(), causing the consensusCount check to be bypassed since the loop only sets CONSENSUS_REACHED on newly qualifying groups, while the pre-set status may confuse downstream verifySignatures() into processing a fraudulent file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a very large collection of StreamFileSignature objects (millions of entries), all with VERIFIED status and unique fileHash values, causing the HashMultimap to consume excessive heap memory and trigger an OutOfMemoryError inside validate(), which prevents the importer from processing any transactions and effectively halts gossip of all stream file transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a collection of StreamFileSignature objects where the first signature has a valid node but subsequent signatures have nodes with getStake() returning negative values, causing the stake accumulation (stake += signature.getNode().getStake()) to produce a negative total, making canReachConsensus() return false and preventing gossip of all transactions in the targeted stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that HashMultimap.create() is used (which allows duplicate values per key) to inject duplicate StreamFileSignature objects with the same fileHash, causing the stake to be double-counted for the same node, artificially inflating the stake for a minority group to exceed stakeRequiredForConsensus and causing a fraudulent CONSENSUS_REACHED status to be set, allowing gossip of a manipulated transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with status VERIFIED and a fileHash that is a very long byte array (e.g., Integer.MAX_VALUE bytes), causing getFileHashAsHex() to produce an extremely long string key in signatureHashMap, leading to excessive memory allocation and a denial-of-service that prevents gossip of all transactions in the current processing batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of cross-signature filename validation in validate() — the filename is only read from the first signature and never verified against subsequent signatures — to inject signatures with mismatched filenames that pass the VERIFIED status check, causing the wrong stream file's transactions to be gossiped or the correct file's transactions to be suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a ConcurrentModificationException inside validate() by passing a collection that is being concurrently modified (e.g., a synchronized list that another thread is modifying), causing the for-each loop over signatures to throw an exception that propagates out of validate() and prevents gossip of all transactions in the targeted stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stakeRequiredForConsensus is computed using RoundingMode.CEILING, meaning that for certain totalStake values the required threshold is rounded up to a value that is strictly greater than 1/3 of totalStake, and by crafting a totalStake value where ceiling rounding pushes the threshold just above the available legitimate stake, prevent consensus from being reached and block gossip of the targeted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with status VERIFIED and a fileHash whose hex representation is identical to the hex of a different byte array (hash collision), causing the attacker's signature to be grouped with legitimate signatures under the same key in signatureHashMap, diluting the stake count for the legitimate group and preventing it from reaching consensus, thus blocking gossip of the targeted transactions?
